title: classifyCurrentSession default branch returns 'no-current' for saving/switching states instead of failing
description: REQ-EPNS-007 states: 'SavingState and SwitchingState are NOT valid inputs; the caller guards against them.' The implementation at classify-current-session.ts lines 51-54 has a default branch that silently returns `{ kind: 'no-current' }` for saving/switching states. This defensive fallback masks caller bugs: if a caller fails to guard against saving/switching, classifyCurrentSession will silently classify the session as 'no-current', leading to the pipeline skipping the flush step entirely and potentially causing data loss (the in-progress save would be ignored). The spec comment at line 51 says 'treat as no-op defensively' but this contradicts REQ-EPNS-007 which says these are NOT valid inputs.
recommendation: Replace the defensive default with an explicit throw: `throw new Error('classifyCurrentSession: invalid state ' + state.status + '. Caller must guard against saving/switching.')`. This makes precondition violations immediately visible rather than silently degrading.
